questions.question: Explain the difference between break and continue statements in Java with an example for each.
keyPoints: Break terminates the loop/switch and transfers control outside
Continue skips current iteration and transfers to next iteration
Example of break: exits loop when condition met
Example of continue: skips specific iteration

questions.question: What are labeled break and labeled continue statements in Java? Explain with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Labeled break exits the specified outer loop
Labeled continue skips to next iteration of specified outer loop
Requires a label identifier before the loop
Example showing nested loop scenario

questions.question: Explain the return statement in Java. Differentiate between returning a value and returning nothing in methods.
keyPoints: Return exits method and transfers control to caller
In void methods: return; without value
In non-void methods: return value;
Cannot have unreachable code after return

questions.question: Write a Java program to demonstrate the use of break statement to find the first number divisible by 7 in an array.
keyPoints: Array declaration and initialization
Loop to traverse array
Break when condition is met
Display result

questions.question: Write a Java program using continue statement to print only odd numbers from 1 to 20.
keyPoints: For loop from 1 to 20
Use continue when number is even
Print odd numbers
Output should show 1,3,5,...19

questions.question: What is the purpose of break statement in a switch case? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Prevents fall-through to next case
Terminates the switch block
Without break, all subsequent cases execute
Example showing with and without break

questions.question: Differentiate between break, continue, and return statements in Java.
keyPoints: Break: exits loop/switch
Continue: skips iteration, continues loop
Return: exits method entirely
Each transfers control to different locations
